01 — About

WHO I AM

I'm Shawon — a marketing undergraduate at the University of Dhaka who lives at the intersection of strategy and execution. From planning to coordinating, I bring both analytical thinking and creative energy to everything I do.

My work spans digital campaigns, event logistics, brand activations, content writing, and corporate sales — giving me a 360° view of how marketing and management actually work on the ground.

04 — Projects

FEATURED PROJECTS

One-Way ANOVA
Statistical Analysis
Crime Rate Variance Across Urban Districts
Can a single geographic factor — district location — statistically explain differences in crime frequency across a city?

Applied One-Way ANOVA to determine whether mean crime rates differ significantly across four districts of the City of Maumee — Rec Center, Key Street, Monclova, and Whitehouse — using six days of recorded data at a 0.05 significance level.

One-Way ANOVA F-Test Significance Level Excel Statistics
Download Excel
→
Two-Way ANOVA
Statistical Analysis
Ad Size & Color Impact on Reader Response
Do two independent factors — advertisement size and color — each independently and jointly influence how magazine readers respond?

Applied Two-Way ANOVA for Shank's Inc. to simultaneously test the effect of ad size (Small, Medium, Large) and color (Red, Blue, Orange, Green) on reader ratings — revealing both main effects and interaction effects between the two variables.

Two-Way ANOVA Interaction Effects Marketing Research Excel Statistics
Download Excel
→

🏛️ Legacy Event

Organizer — MarcVenture

Department of Marketing · University of Dhaka

MarcVenture is a business case competition organized by the Department of Marketing, University of Dhaka, to celebrate its Golden Jubilee — commemorating 50 years of leadership in Marketing education, innovation, and influence. An opportunity for young minds to be part of a legacy.
